info.title: Elvia.Edna.Events.Cli
info.description:
Copyright (C) 2026 Elvia.Edna.Events.Cli
avro-create                    Create an Avro schema for a .NET type.
code-generate                  Generate C# code for a schema from the Schema
Registry or stdin.
registry-get                   Retrieve a schema from the Schema Registry.
registry-test                  Verify that a .NET type is compatible with a
schema in the Schema Registry.
registry-versions-get          Retrieve versions of schema from the Schema
Registry.
registry-delete                Delete schema for a given topic.
kafka-topic-delete             Delete a given topic. Must be recreated
manually in Terraform.
kafka-topic-messages-delete    Delete messages in a given topic. Only if NOT
using compacting.
kafka-metadata-get             List Kafka metadata.
consumergroup-offset-change    Set offset for consumer group
consumergroup-delete           Delete a consumer group
help                           Display more information on a specific command.
version                        Display version information.

options.name: --assembly
options.description:
The name of or path to an assembly to load (multiple
space-separated values accepted).

options.name: --enum-behavior
options.description:
The type of schema that enum types should be
represented by. Options are "symbolic" (generate an
"enum" schema; the default behavior), "integral"
(generate an "int" or "long" schema based on the
underlying type; the behavior for all flag enums),
and "nominal" (generate a "string" schema).

options.name: --nullable-references
options.description:
(Default: Annotated) Which reference types should be
represented with nullable union schemas. Options are
"annotated" (use nullable annotations if available;
the default behavior), "none", and "all".

options.name: --temporal-behavior
options.description:
Whether timestamps should be represented with
"string" schemas (ISO 8601) or "long" schemas
(timestamp logical types). Options are "iso8601",
"epochmilliseconds", and "epochmicroseconds".

options.name: --type
options.description: Required. The type to build a schema for.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

options.name: --nullable-references
options.description:
(Default: true) Whether reference types selected for
nullable record fields should be annotated as
nullable.

options.name: --registry-config
options.description:
Configuration options to provide to the registry
client (multiple space-separated key=value pairs
accepted).

options.name: --registry-url
options.description: The URL of the schema registry.

options.name: --id
options.description:
If a subject/version/topic is not specified, the ID
of the schema.

options.name: --subject
options.description: If an ID is not specified, the subject of the schema.

options.name: --version
options.description: The version of the schema.

options.name: --topic
options.description: The value subject topic name

options.name: --system
options.description:
The system/namespace for the consuming application.
Used for fetching schema registry credentials from
Vault.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --group
options.description:
Required. Consumer group. Your user must have access to the
consumer groups system.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --group

options.name: --offset
options.description:
Required. Comma separated list of partition offsets. Either
position based '124,567' or key/value with partition=offset
'0=124,1=5678'
